problemTitle: All Paths From Source to Target

sources.code:
# Time:  O(p + r * n), p is the count of all the possible paths in graph,
#                      r is the count of the result.
# Space: O(n)

class Solution(object):
    def allPathsSourceTarget(self, graph):
        """
        :type graph: List[List[int]]
        :rtype: List[List[int]]
        """
        def dfs(graph, curr, path, result):
            if curr == len(graph)-1:
                result.append(path[:])
                return
            for node in graph[curr]:
                path.append(node)
                dfs(graph, node, path, result)
                path.pop()

        result = []
        dfs(graph, 0, [0], result)
        return result
sources.originalCode:
# Time:  O(p + r * n), p is the count of all the possible paths in graph,
#                      r is the count of the result.
# Space: O(n)

class Solution(object):
    def allPathsSourceTarget(self, graph):
        """
        :type graph: List[List[int]]
        :rtype: List[List[int]]
        """
        def dfs(graph, curr, path, result):
            if curr == len(graph)-1:
                result.append(path[:])
                return
            for node in graph[curr]:
                path.append(node)
                dfs(graph, node, path, result)
                path.pop()

        result = []
        dfs(graph, 0, [0], result)
        return result
sources.reviewedCode:
# Time:  O(p + r * n), p is the count of all the possible paths in graph,
#                      r is the count of the result.
# Space: O(n)

# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Carry one current path, explore one choice, then cleanly step back before taking the next.
class Solution(object):
    def allPathsSourceTarget(self, graph):
        """
        :type graph: List[List[int]]
        :rtype: List[List[int]]
        """
        def dfs(graph, curr, path, result):
            # [Reader note] State setup
            # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Building paths incrementally, backtracking over graph choices, and recognizing when acyclicity simplifies the search.
            if curr == len(graph)-1:
                result.append(path[:])
                return
            # [Reader note] Main pass
            # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
            for node in graph[curr]:
                path.append(node)
                dfs(graph, node, path, result)
                path.pop()

        result = []
        dfs(graph, 0, [0], result)
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return result

sources.relativePath: solution/0700-0799/0797.All Paths From Source to Target/Solution.py
sources.reviewedCode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Carry one current path, explore one choice, then cleanly step back before taking the next.
class Solution:
    def allPathsSourceTarget(self, graph: List[List[int]]) -> List[List[int]]:
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Building paths incrementally, backtracking over graph choices, and recognizing when acyclicity simplifies the search.
        n = len(graph)
        q = deque([[0]])
        ans = []
        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        while q:
            path = q.popleft()
            u = path[-1]
            # [Reader note] Decision point
            # Pay attention here: The output itself grows, so the focus should stay on path construction discipline rather than on over-optimizing the traversal.
            if u == n - 1:
                ans.append(path)
                continue
            for v in graph[u]:
                q.append(path + [v])
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return ans
